meta.reasoning: As Evil leader on Quest 1, proposing myself + a Good player (s3). Will play success to build trust and establish credibility. Avoid putting both Evil players together early — if quest fails, both get immediately suspected. Better to appear trustworthy now and leverage that later.
As Evil, I support my partner s1's proposal. Team [s1, s3] lets s1 sabotage Quest 1. Approving is also natural behavior that avoids raising suspicion early.
I am Good and I'm on the proposed team. Approving gives us a 2-person quest where I guarantee a success card. Useful early info if the quest fails (meaning s1 is Evil).
First quest, no information yet. Approving gathers information — if it fails, we know one of s1/s3 is evil. Rejecting wastes a rejection slot without benefit.
